content.verdict: Le Meridien Bora Bora sits on Motu Tape on the eastern side of the lagoon with arguably the most dramatic Mount Otemanu view of any Bora Bora resort — Otemanu looms directly across the water from every overwater bungalow. The resort's signature is its lagoonarium, a fenced natural swimming area that functions as a turtle sanctuary where sea turtles are rehabilitated and released. You can snorkel with them, which is the memory most guests take home. 99 overwater bungalows have big glass floor panels (these are the largest glass floors on the island — practically a window to the lagoon from the bed), and the interiors were updated in the last few years to a clean, modern palette. The infinity pool looking straight at Otemanu is a postcard. The beach is good but smaller and more sheltered than at Hilton or Pearl. Service is solid Marriott standard — professional rather than intimate — and pricing is typically 15-25% below Four Seasons and St. Regis for very comparable product. Bonvoy members can redeem points, which makes this the smartest award redemption on Bora Bora. Ideal for honeymooners who prioritize the view of Otemanu and the turtle experience over intimate boutique service.
content.best_room: Book an Otemanu View Overwater Villa — this is the money room, with the mountain filling the entire view from your deck and bed. The Premium Overwater Villa with plunge pool is a worthwhile upgrade for milestone honeymoons. Avoid garden-view categories: the whole point of Le Meridien is the Otemanu panorama.
caveats: The beach is smaller and more protected than at Hilton or Pearl — fine for a swim but not a long beach walk.
Dining can feel corporate compared with boutique alternatives.
Food and drink pricing is high; the half-board package helps.
The turtle sanctuary rehabilitation program occasionally means fewer turtles on-site depending on the release cycle.
Some overwater bungalows back onto the motu itself — specify 'end of pontoon' for deepest water and most privacy.

faqs.a: The lagoonarium is a registered sea turtle rehabilitation program — injured or young turtles are cared for and released once healthy. Marine biologists are on staff, and guests can participate in educational feedings. It is widely considered a good-faith conservation operation, not a captive attraction.

faqs.q: How does Le Meridien compare with Conrad, Hilton, Four Seasons?
faqs.a: Best Otemanu view of any of them. Smaller beach than Hilton/Pearl. Less intimate than Four Seasons. Most points-friendly (Marriott Bonvoy). Good value in the five-star tier.

faqs.q: Can we use Marriott Bonvoy points?
faqs.a: Yes — this is a standard Bonvoy property. Award nights run roughly 85,000-115,000 points for overwater villas. It is one of the most aspirational Bonvoy redemptions globally.
No, children are welcome, but couples dominate and there is no kids' club. The atmosphere is honeymoon-centric.
Otemanu View Overwater Villa, end-of-pontoon if available — maximum water depth, minimum motu interference, signature mountain view.
Closest of the major resorts — 15-minute boat transfer from Motu Mute airport directly to the dock.

Hi reservations,

We would like to book an Otemanu View Overwater Villa, end-of-pontoon if available, for 7 nights arriving [date], for our honeymoon.

Please confirm:
- Availability and best honeymoon rate

Thank you,
[Names]
